notable_results: Grand Tour stage wins across Giro and Vuelta (including Salerno 2023) and the 2024 Vuelta a España points classification title.

notable_results: Regular presence in Grand Tour breakaways with multiple top-10 stage finishes; former Austrian U23 champion.

notable_results: Young Belgian puncheur with solid showings in U23 one-day races and a strong work ethic.

notable_results: Completed multiple Giros with a best of 19th overall; regularly features in mountain breakaways to chase KOM points.

giro_role_2026: Supports the team in controlling the race on flat to rolling terrain, shepherding fellows and chasing moves; also free to pursue breakaways to seek a stage result or soften up the field for teammates.

notable_results: Giro d’Italia 2019 stage winner and 2020 Giro 9th overall; experienced Italian climber regaining form after injury setbacks.
giro_role_2026: Brings veteran climbing depth, assisting Zana and others on the big ascents; if fully fit, he could relaunch his career by featuring in mountain breakaways seeking a home soil stage victory.

notable_results: 4th overall in Tour de France 2021 and stage winner; a proven stage racer with a penchant for long, uphill attacks and strong consistency.
giro_role_2026: Primary GC contender for Jayco, aiming for a podium finish by riding intelligently in the mountains and possibly gaining time through a well-timed attack or two in the high Alps.

notable_results: Two-time Giro stage winner (2019) and 2019 Giro points classification winner; an experienced top-tier sprinter with numerous Grand Tour victories.
giro_role_2026: The team’s marquee sprinter for flat stages, seeking to add to his tally of GT stage wins; will rely on teammates to pilot him into winning positions in bunch sprints.

notable_results: Two Giro stage wins and the Mountains classification in 2022; a deadly breakaway climber and strong lieutenant on tough ascents.
giro_role_2026: Key mountain helper for O’Connor and also opportunistic stage hunter; if freed from duties, will animate breakaways on mountain stages in pursuit of another stage win or KOM points.

notable_results: Vuelta a España stage winner 2021; an experienced road captain known for tireless work and savvy breakaway timing.
giro_role_2026: Road captain keeping the team organized and chasing down dangerous moves; valuable flat and medium-terrain worker and a potential breakaway presence if leaders are secure.

notable_results: Former MTB World Cup star and Commonwealth Games medalist; transitioning to road with strong raw power and bike handling skills.
giro_role_2026: A domestique role across different terrains, helping to position O’Connor on climbs and lead out Ackermann in sprints, while also adjusting to the rigors of Grand Tour racing.

notable_results: British climber in his neo-pro season; impressed with a top-15 in a tough stage of Tour of the Alps 2025.
giro_role_2026: Learns the ropes as a climbing domestique by assisting O’Connor on mountain stages and possibly riding into some breakaways for added experience and team presence.

notable_results: Winner of a 2023 Coppa e Bartali stage; agile German puncheur bridging from U23 success into WorldTour level.
giro_role_2026: Helps cover attacks on rolling terrain and supports the team’s GC and sprint efforts; might seek a break on a medium mountain stage to attempt a surprise stage result.

notable_results: Giro d’Italia 2021 stage winner; crafty Italian puncheur with a fast kick from small groups and adept at hilly breakaways.
giro_role_2026: Functions as both a domestique and a stage opportunist: on some days assisting team leaders, and on others, seeking to repeat his previous Giro stage win by joining the right breakaway.

notable_results: Breakthrough overall victory at 2024 Tour of Turkey and a combative ride in 2024 Tour de France (including a close Stage 1 second place); a promising Dutch stage racer.
giro_role_2026: Co-leads the team hoping to crack the top 10 overall while eyeing a high-mountain stage to showcase his talent; will also contend for the best young rider if in form.

notable_results: Quick Dutch sprinter with multiple u23 and Asian tour wins; building experience in his first Grand Tour sprint fields.
giro_role_2026: Takes on bunch sprints as the team’s finisher, aiming for respectable placings despite limited support and using this Giro to learn the craft of sprinting among the world’s best.

notable_results: Consistent performer in continental circuits; strong helper with a good tempo on flat-to-rolling courses.
giro_role_2026: Provides general support, especially on flats, and will likely commit to early breakaways to represent the team and relieve pressure on his leaders in the peloton.

notable_results: Former mountain biker turned road racer; placed top 10 in some smaller hilltop finishes showing a spark of finishing speed uphill.
giro_role_2026: Support role on rolling stages, chasing moves and protecting leaders; will likely get freedom to go on the offensive on a transition stage to try for a standout result.

notable_results: A stalwart mountain domestique with multiple Giro appearances; guided Romain Bardet in climbs at Team DSM and finished several GTs strongly.
giro_role_2026: Experienced climbing domestique who will shepherd Van den Broek and Leemreize on big ascents and provide steady pacing; also a candidate to mark dangerous breakaways for the team.

notable_results: Made a name with a close 2nd place on a Giro 2022 summit finish at 22; a gifted Dutch climber poised for leadership roles.
giro_role_2026: Co-leader focusing on stage wins in the high mountains and possibly an outside tilt at top-10 GC. If he finds himself with GC time loss early, he will turn to breakaways to aim for a victory.

notable_results: A reliable support rider with a strong engine, gradually stepping up in his support responsibilities; nephew of a famous domestique (Koen de Kort).
giro_role_2026: Carries out fundamental domestique tasks — feeding, bottle fetching, and keeping leaders out of trouble — while possibly joining breakaways that don't threaten the GC to gain personal experience.

notable_results: 2017 Tour de France Mountains classification winner with two stage wins; veteran French climber and former Vuelta 10th overall known for his attacking style.
giro_role_2026: Team’s inspirational leader and stage hunter, aiming to crown his late career with a Giro mountain stage win; he will animate breakaways on difficult stages and mentor his young teammates.

notable_results: A veteran of countless breakaways in the Giro; winner of the intermediate sprints classification in Giro 2017 and a persistent attacker.
giro_role_2026: Will again be a key figure in breakaways, aiming to maximize sponsor exposure and perhaps steal a stage from a surprise move in the final kilometers if bigger teams misjudge the catch.

notable_results: Notorious long-range attacker, having spent multiple days in Giro breakaways in recent editions; known for his fearless attitude up the road.
giro_role_2026: Will aggressively hunt breakaways, particularly in medium mountain or transitional stages, with the ambition of staying away to contend for a stage win from a small group or at least secure a combativity prize.

notable_results: Young Italian with modest palmarès, new to Grand Tours, focusing on learning and helping where possible.
giro_role_2026: Rides in service of the team’s breakaway strategy and overall functioning, while proudly carrying the flag for Malta-based sponsorship; success measured by completing the race and aiding the team’s attacks.

notable_results: Fast finisher with a stage win at 2019 Tour of Thailand and multiple top-10s in European 1.1 races; Polti’s best bet in any bunch sprint.
giro_role_2026: Will try to surf wheels in the chaotic sprints with minimal team support, aiming for top-10 finishes or better if the top sprinters are missing; his goal is to gain exposure and experience in the GT sprint field.

notable_results: Young rider possibly of Maltese nationality given the sponsor; virtually unknown at WT level, indicating a focus on team rather than personal results so far.
giro_role_2026: Rides in service of the team’s breakaway strategy and overall functioning, while proudly carrying the flag for Malta-based sponsorship; success measured by completing the race and aiding the team’s attacks.

notable_results: Up-and-coming Italian who has shown glimpses of talent in domestic races; comfortable in medium mountains and breakaways.
giro_role_2026: Likely to join breakaways on hilly stages, hoping to gain notoriety and possibly contest a stage if the stars align, all while fulfilling any domestique requests from his team leaders.

notable_results: Solid climber who excelled in smaller Italian stage races; relatively new to the top level, but shows resilience on big climbs.
giro_role_2026: Covers climbing domestique duties on mountain stages, possibly forming part of early breakaways to put the team at the forefront; aims to finish strongly in his first Giro experience.

notable_results: A veteran of Spanish domestic scene (ex-EOLO Kometa rider); regularly active in breakaways in races like Tour of Turkey and smaller stage races.
giro_role_2026: Joins the expected breakaway barrage from his team, using his experience to help orchestrate moves; he’ll try to shine for the cameras and aim for personal glory if an escape group stays away.

notable_results: 6th overall in Giro 2023 with a stint in the maglia rosa; time trial national champion and one of Norway’s top stage racing talents.
giro_role_2026: Leads the Uno-X team’s GC hopes, striving to break into the top 5 or higher overall; will ride aggressively if needed and defend strongly to improve upon his past performance, using his TT as a weapon.

notable_results: Promising Norwegian climber with top amateur results; forging his path in his first large stage races as part of Uno-X’s youth movement.
giro_role_2026: Rides as a mountain domestique for Leknessund, doing what he can on earlier gradients; may be given rope to venture into a breakaway on a tough stage to continue learning and trying for an upset.

notable_results: New pro with strong showings in Scandinavian races; a useful all-rounder who can contribute in various situations.
giro_role_2026: Provides support on flat and rolling stages, possibly part of the engine behind controlling breakaways, and looks to finish the race in his first Grand Tour as a learning experience.

notable_results: Up-and-coming Norwegian rider; limited results but part of Uno-X’s development pipeline with a good work ethic.
giro_role_2026: Functions as a domestique on flatter days, helping keep the team leaders safe and fed; the goal is consistent support and finishing his first Grand Tour to mature as a rider.

notable_results: Former U23 Liège winner; strong puncheur with top-10 in 2021 Tour of Flanders and multiple aggressive rides in WorldTour races.
giro_role_2026: A go-to rider for lumpy breakaways and one-day style stages; he will be looking to convert his one-day prowess into a stage win from a break or reduced group, while also serving the team’s sprint or GC efforts as needed.

notable_results: Another young Norwegian domestique forging a path; strong time trial base, indicative of potential in break control roles.
giro_role_2026: Keeps a steady pace for the team in early parts of stages and helps in the team time trial context if necessary; aims to complete the race and gain considerable endurance and insight.

notable_results: Young climber who won the 2022 Tour of Rhodes; stepping up to help the Uno-X cause in bigger races with his climbing legs.
giro_role_2026: Provides assistance on longer climbs in early phases, tries to hang in as deep as possible, and might seize a chance in an escapade on a mountain stage to test himself on the big stage.

notable_results: Norwegian sprinter with a stage win at 2022 Tour of Rhodes; fast finisher but new to top-tier sprint competitions.
giro_role_2026: Will try to contest any bunch sprints for Uno-X as their designated fast man, with a focus on getting into the mix and learning how to navigate Grand Tour speed and positioning.

notable_results: 2022 & 2023 Tour de France champion; the preeminent stage racer of his generation with unmatched climbing prowess and strong time trialing ability.
giro_role_2026: The outright favorite for overall victory. He will look to exert dominance on key mountain stages and maintain (or gain) advantage in the time trial, with his entire team dedicated to protecting and propelling him to his first Giro title.

notable_results: Former hour record holder and European TT champion; a powerhouse known for successful breakaways and high-speed solo efforts.
giro_role_2026: Provides invaluable strength on flat and rolling terrain, controlling breakaways and chasing down threats. Additionally, he may take a shot at the ITT stage for a top finish and possibly attempt a late-stage solo break if circumstances allow.

notable_results: 3rd overall in Giro 2020; a consistent high-mountain support rider and former GC podium finisher with wealth of experience in assisting team leaders.
giro_role_2026: Plays the role of senior mountain domestique for Vingegaard, pacing the peloton on big climbs and reeling in attacks. His presence helps ensure Jonas faces minimal threats until the critical moments.

notable_results: 2023 Vuelta a España overall winner and long-time super-domestique; arguably the best pure climbing helper in the peloton with multiple GT stage wins.
giro_role_2026: A crucial mountain teammate, Kuss will shepherd Vingegaard on the toughest ascents and can shut down rivals’ moves. If Jonas is ever in difficulty, Kuss provides an insurance card with his own high climbing level.

notable_results: Former cyclocross rider now making waves on the road; strong in short bursts and technical courses, still gaining experience in big tours.
giro_role_2026: Supports across medium stages and in hectic moments of flat stages, using his bike handling to keep Jonas safe. If the rare opportunity arises, might jump in a break on a lumpy stage to test his legs.

notable_results: Dutch rider making his GT debut; strong domestique in smaller races, especially on hilly courses and time trials.
giro_role_2026: Provides workforce in controlling breakaways and ensures Vingegaard is well-positioned into climbs and TTs; the main goal for Lemmen is to support and reach the finish line of his first Grand Tour.

notable_results: Promising Italian climber, 3rd in 2022 U23 Giro d’Italia; learning the ropes at WorldTour level and hungry to perform on home roads.
giro_role_2026: An eager climbing domestique who will do his part by carrying out early mountain workload for the team; could be allowed in breakaways to scout the road ahead or to chase personal results if the situation permits.

notable_results: Young Swiss rider and solid all-rounder; limited high-profile results but a consistent performer in U23 ranks.
giro_role_2026: A generalist helper for Vingegaard, ready to assist in the peloton or fetch supplies when needed; gaining important experience in his first GT as part of a high-pressure GC campaign.

notable_results: 2023 Tour de France 3rd overall and 2016 Tour 4th; a seasoned British climber with explosive attacks and proven podium credentials in Grand Tours.
giro_role_2026: Assumes leadership in Pogačar’s absence, targeting a podium finish. He’ll ride conservatively, staying with or near Vingegaard as long as possible and possibly leveraging his quick uphill acceleration to gain time where he can.

notable_results: Two-time Vuelta a España stage winner (2021, 2022) and Australian national TT champion 2023; a strong all-rounder with a remarkable rise from Zwift to the WorldTour.
giro_role_2026: Prime climbing domestique and secondary GC option, he’ll pace Yates on climbs and is capable of stepping up if needed. Additionally, he may target the TT stage for a high result given his prowess against the clock.

notable_results: Vuelta a España stage winner 2022; mercurial Spanish talent known for riding aggressively, sometimes at the expense of consistency, but invaluable as a free-roaming helper.
giro_role_2026: A wildcard mountain lieutenant who can either drop back to help Yates or go up the road in a break to pressure other teams; his unpredictability can be a strategic asset in mountainous stages.

notable_results: Triple U23 World Time Trial Champion (2017-2019) and Tour de France 2023 stage winner; a formidable engine on flats and TTs who also climbs better than many pure TT specialists.
giro_role_2026: Leads out on flat stages and long valley sections with his immense power, protects Yates in winds and technical areas, and will aim for a top performance in the individual time trial stage as a personal goal.

notable_results: 18-year-old Swiss prodigy making an early GT debut; junior world champion in cyclocross and already part of the pro ranks under special development program.
giro_role_2026: Focuses on learning and doing the basics right, like positioning teammates and carrying bottles; any significant contribution is a bonus at his age, with the main goal of reaching Rome to gain invaluable experience.

notable_results: 2022 Junior World Championships road race silver medalist; a highly touted young Portuguese climber in his rookie pro season.
giro_role_2026: Rides in the service of older teammates, especially on uphill terrain; learning the trade in the mountains of Italy and potentially taking a chance in an escape group on a day off for the leaders to stretch his legs.

notable_results: Spanish neo-pro with a strong climbing lineage (son of a pro); has shown promise in U23 stage races with solid climbing performances.
giro_role_2026: Supports on the climbs where possible, carrying out early uphill pacing or recovering from break duties to assist, while gradually building the endurance and skills needed for future leadership roles.

notable_results: Giro d’Italia stage winner 2020; an Ecuadorian workhorse well-versed in breakaways and tough conditions, with both one-day and GT support experience.
giro_role_2026: Joins breakaways strategically to disrupt other teams and lighten the load on UAE’s leaders or to pursue a stage win if he finds the right move; also covers domestique roles on varied terrain given his versatility.

notable_results: 5 Tour de France stage wins and numerous GT sprint victories; one of the most accomplished sprinters of his era with a fearsome finishing kick.
giro_role_2026: Top sprinter looking to assert his dominance in flat finishes. Despite limited support, he has the experience to find position and convert at least one of the Giro’s flat stages into a win, aiming to add to his GT stage tally.

notable_results: 2023 Tour de France stage winner (Mt. Blanc); veteran mountain domestique with a Vuelta KOM title (2017) and countless supportive roles for leaders.
giro_role_2026: Brings a wealth of mountain experience to this wildcard team, guiding younger riders in tough stages. He may get the green light to chase a final career stage win in the mountains via breakaway, but otherwise will serve as a mentor and climbing captain.

notable_results: Fast Dutchman with a 2023 Tour of Estonia overall win and stage wins in 2.1 races; capable of both sprinting and fueling breakaways.
giro_role_2026: Serves as Groenewegen’s de facto lead-out man in the final kilometers despite the relatively lean sprint train, ensuring Dylan is well-positioned; also ready to jump into breakaways on days without a sprint expectation.

notable_results: Solid performances in lower-tier stage races; an all-rounder with a particular knack for breakaways in moderately hilly terrain trying to establish himself.
giro_role_2026: Another likely breakaway candidate on transitional stages, helping animate the race and possibly set up moves for teammates; he’ll also slot into the sprint train as needed for early lead-out duties for Groenewegen.

notable_results: Young Czech-American rider with success in junior ranks; working his way into pro scene with promising versatility.
giro_role_2026: Learns through service, supporting Poels in break management and Groenewegen in chase efforts, while possibly juggling his own chance in a break on an easier day to stretch his legs and show potential.

notable_results: Brother of Matyáš and similarly versatile, with a track background; early in his career but with a solid engine.
giro_role_2026: Aids in team chores across the board, covering echelons or delivering bottles. If both Kopecký brothers are in shape, they might launch a tandem break to lighten the team’s load on any given stage.

notable_results: Slovak sprinter showing glimpses in smaller races; multiple national podiums behind Peter Sagan, indicating finishing speed.
giro_role_2026: Part of Groenewegen’s sprint support crew in the final kilometers, likely his second or third man; ready to step in as a sprinter if Groenewegen has an off day or if a break makes it to the line on a lumpy stage for a reduced sprint.

notable_results: Track Olympic medalist; powerful Dane who has transitioned into a valuable lead-out man with a good sense of timing.
giro_role_2026: Likely the final lead-out rider for Groenewegen’s sprint efforts, using his track-honed speed and technique to drop Dylan off in prime position in the last 150 meters of each mass sprint.

notable_results: 2019 Tour of Flanders champion and Giro d’Italia stage winner in 2021; a crafty racer with a big engine and the ability to attack solo.
giro_role_2026: Top stage hunter for Astana, eyeing rolling stages to attack and finish solo or from a small group. He’ll be given free rein to get in moves and use his tactical acumen to deliver a victory for the team.

notable_results: 8 Giro d’Italia stage wins across his career; a consummate stage hunter with a lethal uphill sprint and vast experience.
giro_role_2026: Co-leader targeting stage wins on medium mountain or hilly finishes; will look to add to his stage tally by timing his attacks brilliantly. He’ll manage his efforts with an eye on opportunistic rather than overall glory, potentially also vying for intermediate points.

notable_results: Omloop Het Nieuwsblad 2021 winner; versatile Italian fast man with a strong kick and some experience leading out bigger sprinters in the past.
giro_role_2026: Astana’s designated sprinter for flat and mildly rolling finishes; he aims to crack the top-5 in bunch sprints and possibly steal a stage if the stars align, despite not being the fastest man in the field.

notable_results: Stage winner at Sibiu Tour 2022; improving Italian climber who took a top-20 overall in the 2023 Tour of the Alps, indicating potential for altitude.
giro_role_2026: Helps Ulissi and Bettiol in the climbs and joins breakaways to set the road for the team’s stage ambitions. He might ride aggressively for a stage chance if the opportunity appears on a day off for the leaders.

notable_results: Young Ecuadorian climber in his Grand Tour debut; turned heads with strong rides in l’Avenir and local high-altitude races in South America.
giro_role_2026: Another climbing asset who provides depth on uphill grades, likely pacing the early slopes for senior teammates; his personal ambition is to survive the three weeks and soak up as much as possible.

notable_results: Belgian classics specialist turning stage racer; known for gritty breakaway attempts in semi-classics and a durable engine.
giro_role_2026: Covers flat and rolling sections to protect his climbers and sprinters; likely part of the group chasing or being in breakaways on days that aren’t mountainous as per team strategy, helping to spring teammates like Ulissi or Bettiol in the right moment.

notable_results: Italian fastman with stage wins in the Tour of Qinghai Lake; a capable sprinter now aligned as Ballerini’s lieutenant in the finals.
giro_role_2026: Main lead-out for Ballerini, guiding him through chaotic finales. If Ballerini falters, Malucelli can himself sprint for minor placings or a long-shot podium, making him a double-edged sword in Astana’s sprint strategy.

notable_results: Young Argentinian (or Spanish) rider; relatively unknown to broad audiences, indicating a focus on team rather than personal results so far.
giro_role_2026: Completes the roster as a helper in multiple capacities: assisting in break control on flat days, and simply aiming to finish the race to gain experience. Success for him is growth and any contributions to team victories.
